summary: Excellent soundboard remaster captures a high‑energy 1985 show highlighted by a scorching Scarlet>Eyes>GDTRFB sequence and tight Dancin’ opener.
review: The Miller‑remastered soundboard delivers crisp, sparkly audio that lets the band’s 1985 fire shine through. Reviewers rave about Jerry’s ferocious guitar work, the seamless Scarlet→Eyes transition, and a setlist packed with standout moments from Dancin’ to Truckin’ and The Wheel.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (8/10) are strongly positive, praising both the musical performance and the high‑quality soundboard recording; only one review is negative about song choices, so a high rating with strong confidence is warranted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, sparkly, clean and well‑balanced; Miller’s remaster praised for preserving clarity without any noted dropouts or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers highlight a strong, well‑sequenced setlist with smooth transitions (especially Scarlet→Eyes) and an energetic second set that keeps the momentum high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for tearing it up on guitar, bright tones and sharp playing; even when vocals were strained his lead work remained a highlight.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as “up” in the mix, delivering strong bass presence after Brent’s addition to the band.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir is mentioned positively on Truckin’/TOO and Around >Saturday Night, showing solid rhythm guitar and vocal contributions.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards receive positive nods as part of the refreshed 1985 sound.
